title: Fruity Chewy Japanese Water Chestnut Stew
description: A citrusy, chewy stew that soothes hangovers, featuring water chestnuts simmered in dashi with orange zest and mirin.
instructions: Bring dashi stock to a gentle simmer in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot.
Add sliced water chestnuts, julienned carrot, and sliced shiitake mushrooms, cooking until vegetables soften about 10 minutes.
Stir in diced onion, dried kelp, and a pinch of salt, letting flavors meld for 5 minutes.
Pour in soy sauce, mirin, and sugar, then reduce heat to low, covering the pot, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove kelp, then add orange zest and lemon juice, stirring to combine for 2 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning with more soy sauce or mirin if needed, then simmer uncovered for 3 minutes.
Sprinkle chopped green onion over the stew, allowing it to wilt for 1 minute.
Serve hot straight from the ttukbaegi, spooning onto a bowl with a little extra citrus zest on top.
